prompt:
Your task is to identify likely causal genes within a locus for a given GWAS phenotype. From the list, provide only the likely causal gene (matching one of the given genes). 
Identify the causal gene.
GWAS phenotype: Metabolite levels (N-acetylasparagine)
Genes in locus: {ACTG2},{ALMS1},{BOLA3},{C2orf78},{CCT7},{DGUOK},{DUSP11},{EGR4},{FBXO41},{MOB1A},{MTHFD2},{NAT8},{NAT8B},{NOTO},{PRADC1},{SMYD5},{STAMBP},{TET3},{TPRKB}

raw_response:
Thinking/reasoning:
N-acetylasparagine is an N-acetylated amino acid. Among the listed genes, NAT8 and NAT8B encode N-acetyltransferase enzymes; NAT8 is well-known to acetylate amino acids and has been implicated in GWAS for N-acetylated metabolites. Therefore NAT8 is the most likely causal gene.

Plan:
1. [ ] Review candidate gene functions to identify enzymes that can produce N-acetylasparagine.
2. [ ] Select the most likely causal gene from the list.

Updating plan after steps:
1. [✓] Review candidate gene functions to identify enzymes that can produce N-acetylasparagine (completed)
   - NAT8/NAT8B are N-acetyltransferases; NAT8 has prior links to N-acetylated metabolite genetics.
2. [✓] Select the most likely causal gene from the list (completed)
   - Chosen gene: NAT8

<solution>NAT8</solution>
